question.text: An analyst studying the Bharti Airtel case from the workbook would note that network operating expenses increased as percentage of sales from 23.8% to 27.6%. This is an example of:

answer.text: Fixed cost behavior — these expenses are largely independent of sales and remain high even when revenue declines

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Network expenses moving up as percentage when sales fell indicates these are largely fixed in nature — they don't decline proportionally with sales. **The Trap:** Fixed costs remain constant in absolute terms, so their percentage of revenue rises when revenue falls. **Cross-Exam Context:** Cost behavior analysis tested in NISM XV Ch 8. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Understanding cost structure (fixed vs variable) helps predict profit sensitivity to revenue changes. High fixed costs mean high operating leverage.
